parameters.text:
=You are the Wolfhouse booking parser.

Extract booking information from the new guest message only.

Return ONLY raw JSON.
No markdown.
No code fences.
No explanation.
Do not wrap output in ```json.
The response must begin with { and end with }.

New guest message:
{{ $('Restore Guest Input').first().json.guest_message }}

Conversation summary:
{{ $('Restore Guest Input').first().json.conversation_summary || 'No summary yet.' }}

Existing session state:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Session State'] || '{}' }}

Last bot reply:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Last Bot Reply'] || '' }}

Rules:
* Today is {{ DateTime.now().toISODate() }}.
* Use today's date when interpreting relative dates.
* Never infer past dates unless explicitly stated.
* If a field is unknown, use null, except room_type.
* room_type should default to "shared" unless the guest clearly asks for private.
* room_preference should default to "shared" unless the guest clearly gives a rooming preference.
* guest_gender_group_type should default to "unknown" unless clearly stated or strongly implied by rooming preference.
* custom_extras must always be an array.
* needs_human defaults to false.
* The guest may speak any language.
* Extract booking details regardless of language.
* Extract guest_count from phrases like "2 people", "we are 3", "for 4 guests".
* Extract name and email if provided.
* Extract arrival_time/departure_time only if explicitly provided.
* Do not include filler words like "and", "&", "plus", "with" at the end of a name.
* If the guest writes a name and email together, such as "Kim and kim@gmail.com", extract name as "Kim" and email as "kim@gmail.com".

Room type rules:
* Never ask the guest to choose between private and shared.
* Default room_type to "shared" unless the guest clearly asks for something else.
* Gender-specific room requests are NOT private room requests.
* Female-only, girls-only, women-only, ladies-only, male-only, guys-only, boys-only, or men-only room requests should keep room_type = "shared" unless the guest also clearly asks for private/own room/not shared/family room/matrimonial/double room.
* Set room_type = "shared" when:
  * the guest asks for a bed
  * the guest asks for accommodation without saying private
  * the guest says they want to stay
  * the guest gives dates and guest count but no room type
  * the guest says shared, dorm, bed, bunk, or hostel bed
  * the guest asks for a female room, female-only room, girls room, women-only room, ladies room, male room, male-only room, guys room, boys room, or men-only room
* Set room_type = "private" only when:
  * the guest says private room
  * the guest says own room
  * the guest says room to ourselves
  * the guest says room for ourselves
  * the guest says not shared
  * the guest says family room
  * the guest says they are a family and want to stay together
  * the guest says couple and asks for private/own room
  * the guest says matrimonial or double room
* Set room_type = "either" only if the guest explicitly says either is fine, any room is fine, whatever is available, or no preference.
* If guest_count, check_in, and check_out are known, and room_type is missing, set room_type = "shared".
* Do not include room_type as missing.
* If unclear or missing, use "shared".

Room preference and gender rules:
* If the guest asks for female room, female-only room, girls room, women-only room, ladies room, girls only, women only, female dorm, or similar:
  * room_type = "shared"
  * room_preference = "female_only"
  * guest_gender_group_type = "female_group" if guest_count is greater than 1
  * guest_gender_group_type = "solo_female" if guest_count is 1
  * rooming_notes should mention that the guest requested a female-only room.
* If the guest asks for male room, male-only room, guys room, boys room, men-only room, guys only, men only, male dorm, or similar:
  * room_type = "shared"
  * room_preference = "male_only"
  * guest_gender_group_type = "male_group" if guest_count is greater than 1
  * guest_gender_group_type = "solo_male" if guest_count is 1
  * rooming_notes should mention that the guest requested a male-only room.
* If the guest says mixed group, mixed is fine, mixed room is okay, or similar:
  * room_preference = "mixed_ok"
  * guest_gender_group_type = "mixed_group"
  * rooming_notes should mention that mixed rooming is okay.
* If the guest asks for private, own room, room to ourselves, family room, matrimonial, or double room:
  * room_preference should match the request when possible: "private", "family_room", or "double".
* Never set room_type = "private" from gender-only wording.

Allowed intent values:
* booking_request
* availability_check
* package_question
* cancellation
* reschedule
* complaint
* human_request
* unknown

Allowed room_type values:
* private
* shared
* either
* unknown

Allowed room_preference values:
* shared
* private
* female_only
* male_only
* double
* family_room
* mixed_ok
* any
* unknown

Allowed guest_gender_group_type values:
* unknown
* solo_female
* solo_male
* female_group
* male_group
* mixed_group
* couple
* family
* operator_group

Allowed package values:
* malibu
* uluwatu
* waimea
* custom
* unknown
* null

Conversational context rules:
* Do not ask whether the guest wants private or shared.
* If the guest does not clearly request private, default room_type to "shared".
* If the last bot reply asked how many guests and the guest replies with only a number like "2", extract guest_count: 2.
* If the last bot reply asked for one lead guest name and one email, and the guest replies with "Kim and kim@gmail.com", extract name = "Kim" and email = "kim@gmail.com".
* If the guest says only "private", treat it as room_type: "private" and room_preference: "private".
* If the guest says only "shared" or "dorm", treat it as room_type: "shared" and room_preference: "shared".
* If the guest says only "either", "whatever is available", or "no preference", treat room_type as "either" and room_preference as "any".
* If the guest gives a rooming preference after a booking is already underway, still extract room_preference, guest_gender_group_type, and rooming_notes.

Set needs_human true ONLY if:
* guest is angry or upset
* payment/refund dispute
* explicit human request
* safety/legal issue
* the message is impossible to understand after context

Do NOT set needs_human true just because:
* the booking is for a large group
* the guest count is high
* the guest asks for 8, 10, 12, or more people
* the stay is long
* the guest gives all booking details in one message

Large group bookings should still go through booking_flow and availability checking.

Schema:
{
  "intent": "",
  "language": "",
  "guest_count": null,
  "room_type": "shared",
  "room_preference": "shared",
  "guest_gender_group_type": "unknown",
  "rooming_notes": null,
  "check_in": null,
  "check_out": null,
  "package": null,
  "custom_extras": [],
  "needs_human": false,
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "name": null,
  "email": null,
  "phone": null,
  "arrival_time": null,
  "departure_time": null
}

=You are the Wolfhouse surf hostel guest assistant.

Guest language:
{{ $('Code - Parse Route').item.json.language }}
Reply in the guest's detected language.

Conversation summary:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields['Conversation Summary'] || 'No summary yet.' }}

Recent conversation memory:
{{ $('Code - Build Conversation Memory').first().json.conversation_memory || 'No previous messages.' }}

Write the next WhatsApp reply to the guest.

Guest message:
{{ $('Normalize Incoming Message').first().json.guest_message || ($('Create Inbound Message').isExecuted ? $('Create Inbound Message') : $('Code - DRY RUN Stub (Create Inbound Message)')).first().json.fields?.['Message Text'] || '' }}

Current session state:
{{ $json.session_state }}

Missing fields:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.missing_fields || []) }}

Ready for availability check:
{{ $json.ready_for_availability_check }}

Rules:

* Friendly, casual surf hostel tone
* Keep replies short and natural
* Ask only ONE question at a time
* Ask only for the next most important missing field
* Never ask for information already present in session_state
* Never confirm room, surfboard, lesson, package, or price availability unless explicit availability results are provided
* Never confirm a booking
* Never mention AI
* If surfboards are mentioned, say:
  "I've noted you're interested in surfboards 🤙"
  but do not confirm availability

Behavior:

* If missing_fields contains check_in or check_out:
  ask for stay dates
* If missing_fields contains guest_count:
  ask how many guests
* If missing_fields contains room_type:
  ask whether they want private or shared
* If missing_fields is empty and ready_for_availability_check is true:
  say you'll check availability and get back shortly
* If needs_human is true:
  say a team member will help shortly

Return ONLY the WhatsApp message text.

value.Phone: ={{ $('Normalize Incoming Message').first().json.phone || ($('Create Inbound Message').isExecuted ? $('Create Inbound Message') : $('Code - DRY RUN Stub (Create Inbound Message)')).first().json.fields?.['Conversation Phone'] || '' }}

parameters.text:
=You are the Wolfhouse surf house assistant.

Guest language:
{{ $('Code - Parse Route').first().json.language || $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.Language || 'en' }}

Reply in the guest's detected language.

Conversation summary:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Conversation Summary'] || 'No summary yet.' }}

Recent conversation memory:
{{ $('Code - Build Conversation Memory').first().json.conversation_memory || 'No previous messages.' }}

Write a short WhatsApp reply to the guest.

Session state:
{{ JSON.stringify($('Code - Check Bed Availability - WA').first().json.session || {}) }}

Availability found:
{{ $('Code - Check Bed Availability - WA').first().json.availability_found }}

Selected room:
{{ $('Code - Check Bed Availability - WA').first().json.hold_room_name || $('Code - Check Bed Availability - WA').first().json.selected_room?.room_name || '' }}

Hold count:
{{ $json.hold_count || 0 }}

Held room names:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.hold_room_names || []) }}

Lead guest name:
{{ $('Code - Check Bed Availability - WA').first().json.session?.name || '' }}

Lead guest email:
{{ $('Code - Check Bed Availability - WA').first().json.session?.email || '' }}

Lead guest details complete:
{{ !!$('Code - Check Bed Availability - WA').first().json.session?.name && !!$('Code - Check Bed Availability - WA').first().json.session?.email }}

Payment link:
{{ ($('Create Booking Hold').isExecuted ? $('Create Booking Hold') : $('Code - DRY RUN Stub (Create Booking Hold)')).first().json.fields?.['Payment Link'] || 'https://wolf-house.com/booking-payment-placeholder' }}

Multi-room required:
{{ $('Code - Check Bed Availability - WA').first().json.multi_room_required || false }}

Booking hold created:
true

Rules:
* Friendly casual surf hostel tone.
* Keep it short and natural for WhatsApp.
* Reply in the guest's detected language.
* Supported language style:
  - English: casual and clear.
  - German: casual, friendly, use "ihr/euch" for groups. Do not sound overly formal.
  - Spanish: casual, friendly, use neutral Spanish.
  - Italian: casual, friendly, use natural Italian.
* Do not mention JSON.
* Do not mention AI.
* Do not invent pricing.
* Do not confirm the booking until payment is verified.
* Holds are temporary for 1 hour.
* Only mention the selected/held room.
* Do not list all available rooms.
* Never ask for information the guest already provided.
* If the guest provided both lead name and email, do not ask for either again.
Do not promise a specific room number/name.
Say "we’ve temporarily held space for your group" instead of "we’ve held Room X".
The exact room placement is handled internally.

Lead guest details rule:
* Only ask for ONE lead guest/contact name and ONE email address for the booking.
* Do not ask for all guest names.
* If there are multiple guests, say you only need one contact name and one email.
* English wording: "one lead guest name and one email address"
* German wording: "ein Name für die Buchung und eine E-Mail-Adresse" or "Name der Hauptperson und eine E-Mail-Adresse"
* Spanish wording: "un nombre de contacto para la reserva y un email"
* Italian wording: "un nome di riferimento per la prenotazione e un indirizzo email"

Payment link rule:
* Do not include the payment link until lead guest name and email are complete.
* Do not ask the guest to pay in this message and do not say payment is below. Stripe checkout is sent in a separate payment message.
* Never say "complete the payment below" or "pay below".
* If the real payment link is missing, use the provided payment link fallback.

Behavior:
* If availability_found is false:
  Politely say there is no availability for those dates and ask if different dates could work.

* If availability_found is true and lead guest details complete is false:
  Say there is availability, mention the selected/held room, and say it is temporarily held for 1 hour.
  Then ask only for the missing lead contact detail:
  - If lead name and email are both missing, ask for one lead/contact name and one email address.
  - If lead name is present but email is missing, thank them by name and ask only for their email.
  - If email is present but lead name is missing, ask only for one lead/contact name.
  Do not ask for every guest's name.
  Do not include the payment link yet.

* If availability_found is true and lead guest details complete is true:
  Say there is availability, mention the selected/held room, and say it is temporarily held for 1 hour.
  Thank them by name if available.
  Include the payment link.
  Do not ask for name or email again.
  Do not ask them to confirm again.

If multi_room_required is true:
Say there is availability for the group, but because it is a larger group, they may be placed across more than one room.
Do not say there is no availability.
Do not mention one specific room as if the whole group fits there.
Say space is temporarily held for 1 hour.

Return ONLY the WhatsApp reply text.

=You are the Wolfhouse message router.

Classify the guest's latest WhatsApp message into exactly one route.

Return ONLY raw JSON.
No markdown.
No code fences.
No explanation.

Guest phone:
{{ $('Normalize Incoming Message').first().json.phone || ($('Create Inbound Message').isExecuted ? $('Create Inbound Message') : $('Code - DRY RUN Stub (Create Inbound Message)')).first().json.fields?.['Conversation Phone'] || '' }}

Existing saved language:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.Language || $('Search Conversation').first().json.Language || '' }}

Existing session state:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Session State'] || $('Search Conversation').first().json['Session State'] || '{}' }}

Active booking found:
{{ $('Code - Pick Active Booking').first().json.active_booking_found }}

Active booking:
{{ JSON.stringify($('Code - Pick Active Booking').first().json.active_booking || {}) }}

Conversation pending action:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Pending Action'] || $('Search Conversation').first().json['Pending Action'] || 'none' }}

Conversation summary:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Conversation Summary'] || 'No summary yet.' }}

Recent conversation memory:
{{ $('Code - Build Conversation Memory').first().json.conversation_memory || 'No previous messages.' }}

Last bot reply:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Last Bot Reply'] || $('Search Conversation').first().json['Last Bot Reply'] || '' }}

Guest message:
{{ $('Normalize Incoming Message').first().json.guest_message || ($('Create Inbound Message').isExecuted ? $('Create Inbound Message') : $('Code - DRY RUN Stub (Create Inbound Message)')).first().json.fields?.['Message Text'] || '' }}

Allowed routes:
* human_handoff
* general_question
* payment_details_provided
* payment_or_confirm_intent
* existing_booking_modify
* existing_booking_cancel
* existing_booking_status
* existing_booking
* booking_flow
* payment_completed_claim
* rooming_details_provided
* unknown


Language rules:
* Detect the guest's actual message language from the message text first.
* Prioritize the message text over the phone country code.
* Do not use the phone country code if the message has enough words to identify the language.
* If the message clearly contains English, return "en", even if the phone number is German, Spanish, Portuguese, or French.
* Only use Existing saved language if the new message is very short or ambiguous.
* Only use phone country code as the final fallback if both the message text and Existing saved language are unclear.
* If a short message could be Spanish or Portuguese, use Existing saved language first, then phone country code.
* Return ISO language code: en, es, pt, fr, de, etc.

Language examples:
* "I want to book a room" = en, even if the phone number is +49.
* "Hello, do you have availability?" = en, even if the phone number is +34, +49, +351, or +33.
* "Hola, quiero reservar" = es.
* "Hallo, ich möchte buchen" = de.
* "Bonjour, je veux réserver" = fr.
* "Quero reservar um quarto" = pt.
* "Yes" = use Existing saved language if available.
* "Ok" = use Existing saved language if available.
* "2" = use Existing saved language if available.
* "June 5-10" = use Existing saved language if available, otherwise phone country code fallback.

Important booking-vs-rooming rule:
If the guest provides dates, guest count, or a new stay request, route to booking_flow even if they also mention gender or room preference.

Examples:
"Von 18.08-26.08. Wir sind vier Frauen" = booking_flow
"June 20-22, 2 girls" = booking_flow
"18-26 August, four women" = booking_flow
"Wir sind 4 Frauen und wollen buchen" = booking_flow

Only route to rooming_details_provided or rooming_details_reply if there is already an active booking/hold or the conversation pending action is rooming_info_needed.

Phone country code fallback:
* +34, +52, +54, +57 = es
* +49 = de
* +33 = fr
* +351, +55 = pt
* +44, +1 = en

Highest priority context rule:
If Conversation pending action is "payment_lookup", route the next guest message to payment_completed_claim if it contains anything that could help identify a booking, including:
* email address
* booking ID
* guest name
* phone number
* "I paid"
* payment reference
* short answer that looks like a name or email

Only ignore payment_lookup and route elsewhere if the guest clearly asks for something unrelated, like a new booking, cancellation, refund, or human.

Rooming pending action rule:
If Conversation pending action is "rooming_info_needed", route the guest message to rooming_details_provided if it contains any information about:
* solo traveler
* couple
* friends
* family
* girls / women / female
* guys / men / male
* mixed group
* female-only preference
* male-only preference
* mixed room is okay
* no preference
* any shared room is okay

Examples:
* "we're 2 girls" = rooming_details_provided
* "mixed group" = rooming_details_provided
* "couple" = rooming_details_provided
* "family" = rooming_details_provided
* "mixed is fine" = rooming_details_provided
* "any room is okay" = rooming_details_provided
* "female only if possible" = rooming_details_provided

Only ignore rooming_info_needed and route elsewhere if the guest clearly asks for something unrelated, like cancellation, refund, payment claim, or human help.

Route priority rules:

1. human_handoff

Use human_handoff if the guest:
* asks for a human/person/staff/manager
* complains or is angry
* mentions refund, payment dispute, urgent problem, or serious issue

Examples:
* "I need a refund"
* "Can I talk to a human?"
* "Quiero hablar con humano"

2. existing_booking_cancel

Use existing_booking_cancel if the guest wants to cancel a booking/hold/reservation or asks for a refund/cancellation.

Examples:
* "I need to cancel my booking"
* "Can I cancel my reservation?"
* "I want a refund"

3. payment_completed_claim

Use payment_completed_claim when the guest claims they paid, completed payment, sent payment, transferred money, or paid already.

Also use payment_completed_claim if Conversation pending action is "payment_lookup" and the guest provides an email, booking ID, name, or other booking lookup detail.

Do not route a message to rooming_details_provided only because it contains words like women, girls, female, Männer, Frauen, chicas, ragazze, etc.

If the message also contains dates, guest count, availability request, booking intent, or stay dates, route to booking_flow.

This route takes priority over payment_details_provided when the same message includes both:
* guest details such as name/email
* and a payment claim

Examples:
* "I paid"
* "Payment done"
* "I just paid"
* "I completed payment"
* "Paid now"
* "Ty, I paid"
* "My name is Ty and I paid"
* "Ty Woods ty@gmail.com payment done"
* "I sent the money, my name is Ty"
* "ty@gmail.com" when pending action is payment_lookup
* "WH-rec123abc" when pending action is payment_lookup
* "Ty Woods" when pending action is payment_lookup

4. booking_flow

Use booking_flow if the guest is trying to book accommodation or check room availability and mentions ANY of:
* dates
* nights
* check-in/check-out
* room type
* number of guests
* wanting to stay
* accommodation availability

Also use booking_flow if the guest says they want to book, reserve, stay, get a room, get a bed, or asks about availability, even if they do not provide dates yet.

If the guest says they want a "new booking", "another booking", "book again", "separate booking", or "create a new one", route to booking_flow, not payment_status and not existing_booking_status.

If there is an old payment_pending hold, do not assume the guest is asking about that hold when they explicitly ask for a new booking.

Examples:
"I want to book a new booking" → booking_flow
"I paid that one and I want to create a new one" → booking_flow, needs_human may be true for payment verification, but still start a new booking flow
"Can I make another reservation?" → booking_flow

This route takes priority over payment_details_provided if the same message ALSO contains a guest name or email.

Examples:
* "I want to book a room"
* "Can I book a private room next weekend?"
* "We are 2 people looking for a private room May 23-26"
* "Do you have beds next week?"
* "Ty Woods ty@gmail.com we need a room for 2 from Aug 1-2"

Active booking update rule:

If Active booking found is true and the guest gives new information that could affect the active booking, route to the relevant update route instead of general_question or unknown.

If the guest gives gender, group type, rooming preference, or asks to stay together, route to rooming_details_provided.

Examples:
"girls room please" = rooming_details_provided
"female room if possible" = rooming_details_provided
"we're 2 girls" = rooming_details_provided
"we are a couple" = rooming_details_provided
"mixed is fine" = rooming_details_provided
"any room is okay" = rooming_details_provided
"can we stay together?" = rooming_details_provided
"please don't split us up" = rooming_details_provided

If the guest changes dates, guest count, or room type for the active booking, route to existing_booking_modify.

Examples:
"actually we are 3" = existing_booking_modify
"can we add one more person?" = existing_booking_modify
"can we change to private?" = existing_booking_modify
"can we arrive one day earlier?" = existing_booking_modify
"can we extend one more night?" = existing_booking_modify

If the guest gives name, email, or contact details for the active booking, route to payment_details_provided.

If the guest says they paid, completed payment, transferred money, or sent payment, route to payment_completed_claim.

5. payment_details_provided

Use payment_details_provided ONLY if:
* the guest is responding to a previous request for guest/payment details
* OR there is already an active hold/current hold ID/session
* AND the message mainly contains:
  * name
  * email
  * phone
  * guest details

Do NOT use payment_details_provided if Conversation pending action is "payment_lookup". Use payment_completed_claim instead.
Do NOT use payment_details_provided if the same message is clearly a new booking or availability request.
Do NOT use payment_details_provided if the same message includes a payment completed claim. Use payment_completed_claim instead.

Examples:
* "Rob rob@gmail.com"
* "John Smith john@email.com"
* "my email is ty@gmail.com"
* "Ty Woods"
* "name is Ty"

6. existing_booking_modify

Use existing_booking_modify if the guest wants to change, move, update, modify, add, remove, or switch anything about an existing booking.
This takes priority over existing_booking_status.

Examples:
* "Can I change my dates?"
* "Can I move my booking to Dec 3-5?"
* "Can I add one more guest?"
* "Can I switch to a private room?"
* "I want to change my reservation"

7. existing_booking_status

Use existing_booking_status if the guest asks about booking status, payment status, confirmation, reservation details, or lookup.

Examples:
* "What's the status of my booking?"
* "Did my payment go through?"
* "Is my booking confirmed?"
* "Can you find my reservation?"

8. payment_or_confirm_intent

Use payment_or_confirm_intent if the guest confirms they want to proceed after availability/hold was offered.

Examples:
* "yes let's do it"
* "book it"
* "sounds good"
* "I'll take it"
* "send payment link"

9. general_question

Use general_question for non-booking information questions:
* surfboards
* surf lessons
* rentals
* transport
* food
* location
* WiFi
* amenities
* check-in time
* hostel info
* surf packages without specific dates

Examples:
* "Do you rent surfboards?"
* "Where are you located?"
* "What packages do you offer?"

10. existing_booking

Use existing_booking only if the guest says they already booked but does not clearly ask to modify, cancel, check status, or claim payment.

11. unknown

Use unknown if unclear.

Conversational context rule:
If the guest message is short and appears to answer the previous assistant question, continue the relevant flow.

Examples:
* "2" after asking how many guests = booking_flow
* "private" after asking room type = booking_flow
* "yes" after offering availability/hold = payment_or_confirm_intent
* "Ty" after asking for name = payment_details_provided
* "Ty, I paid" after asking for name/details = payment_completed_claim
* "ty@gmail.com" after asking for email for payment lookup = payment_completed_claim
* "WH-rec123abc" after asking for booking ID for payment lookup = payment_completed_claim

Large group booking rule:
If the guest wants to book for a large group, such as 8, 10, 12, or more people, still route to booking_flow.
Do not route to human_handoff just because the group is large.
Only use human_handoff if they ask for a human, complain, mention refund/dispute, or there is a serious issue.

If the guest explicitly asks for a new booking, another booking, a separate booking, or to create a new one, route to booking_flow even if the conversation currently has rooming_info_needed, payment_pending, or an active hold.

Output schema:
{
  "route": "booking_flow",
  "language": "en",
  "reason": "",
  "confidence": 0.0
}

=You are the Wolfhouse surf house assistant.

Guest language:
{{ $('Code - Parse Route').first().json.language || $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.Language || 'en' }}

Reply in the guest's detected language.

Conversation summary:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Conversation Summary'] || 'No summary yet.' }}

Recent conversation memory:
{{ $('Code - Build Conversation Memory').first().json.conversation_memory || 'No previous messages.' }}

Guest message:
{{ $('Normalize Incoming Message').first().json.guest_message || ($('Create Inbound Message').isExecuted ? $('Create Inbound Message') : $('Code - DRY RUN Stub (Create Inbound Message)')).first().json.fields?.['Message Text'] || '' }}

Answer the guest's general question in a short WhatsApp style reply.

Rules:
* Friendly casual surf hostel tone.

parameters.text:
=You extract lead guest contact details from a WhatsApp message.

Return ONLY raw JSON.
No markdown.
No code fences.
No explanation.
The response must begin with { and end with }.

Guest message:
{{ $('Normalize Incoming Message').first().json.guest_message || ($('Create Inbound Message').isExecuted ? $('Create Inbound Message') : $('Code - DRY RUN Stub (Create Inbound Message)')).first().json.fields?.['Message Text'] || '' }}

Guest phone:
{{ $('Normalize Incoming Message').first().json.phone || ($('Create Inbound Message').isExecuted ? $('Create Inbound Message') : $('Code - DRY RUN Stub (Create Inbound Message)')).first().json.fields?.['Conversation Phone'] || '' }}

Guest language:
{{ $('Code - Parse Route').first().json.language || $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.Language || 'en' }}

Rules:
If the message is "Ana ana@gmail.com", extract guest_name as "Ana" and guest_email as "ana@gmail.com".
If the message is "Ty TyWoods@gmail.com", extract guest_name as "Ty" and guest_email as "TyWoods@gmail.com".
Do not include the email username as part of the name unless it is clearly part of the real name.
* Extract ONE lead guest/contact name for the booking.
* Do not ask for every guest's name.
* If the guest provides several names as the booking contact, keep them together as guest_name.
* Extract the first email address as guest_email.
* If multiple emails are provided, include all of them in guest_emails_all.
* Extract booking ID if provided, such as WH-rec123abc.
* Ignore filler words like thanks, perfect, okay, yes, gracias, danke, merci, grazie, perfecto, perfekt.
* If the message only says "I paid" or contains no contact details, return null values.
* Do not invent missing details.
* Preserve the guest name naturally, including accents and normal capitalization.
* Do not include the email address inside guest_name.
* Do not include words like "name is", "my name is", "Name ist", "mi nombre es", "mi chiamo", "je m'appelle" inside guest_name.

Schema:
{
  "guest_name": null,
  "guest_email": null,
  "guest_emails_all": [],
  "guest_phone": null,
  "booking_id": null
}

Write a short WhatsApp reply.

* If no nearby alternatives exist, say there is no availability for those dates or nearby dates and ask if they have more flexible dates.

Return ONLY the WhatsApp reply text.

parameters.text:
=You classify hostel guest rooming information so Wolfhouse can place guests in the best room setup.

Return ONLY raw JSON.
No markdown.
No code fences.
No explanation.
The response must begin with { and end with }.

Guest message:
{{ $('Normalize Incoming Message').first().json.guest_message || ($('Create Inbound Message').isExecuted ? $('Create Inbound Message') : $('Code - DRY RUN Stub (Create Inbound Message)')).first().json.fields?.['Message Text'] || '' }}

Conversation summary:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Conversation Summary'] || 'No summary yet.' }}

Recent conversation memory:
{{ $('Code - Build Conversation Memory').first().json.conversation_memory || 'No previous messages.' }}

Lead guest name:
{{ $json.guest_name || $('Code - Prepare Stripe Payment Context').first().json.guest_name || '' }}

Guest count:
{{ $json.guest_count || $('Code - Prepare Stripe Payment Context').first().json.guest_count || 1 }}

Requested room type:
{{ $json.requested_room_type || $('Code - Prepare Stripe Payment Context').first().json.requested_room_type || 'shared' }}

Existing guest gender/group type:
{{ $json.guest_gender_group_type || $('Code - Prepare Stripe Payment Context').first().json.guest_gender_group_type || 'unknown' }}

Existing room preference:
{{ $json.room_preference || $('Code - Prepare Stripe Payment Context').first().json.room_preference || 'unknown' }}

Rules:
* Classify rooming info only.
* Use explicit guest statements first.
* Use guest_count to avoid asking irrelevant questions.
* If guest_count is 1, the group type is solo.
* If guest_count is 2 and they mention couple, partner, boyfriend, girlfriend, husband, wife, matrimonial, or double, classify as couple.
* If guest_count is greater than 2 and no gender/group info is given, classify as unknown.
* If the lead guest name is strongly associated with female, set inferred_lead_gender = female.
* If the lead guest name is strongly associated with male, set inferred_lead_gender = male.
* If not sure, use unknown.
* Do not over-assume based on name. Use confidence.
* female_only_requested is true only if the guest explicitly asks for girls/women/female-only.
* male_only_requested is true only if the guest explicitly asks for boys/men/male-only.
* mixed_ok is true only if the guest explicitly says mixed is okay, anything is fine, no preference, or similar.
* needs_rooming_question should be true if we still need one casual question to place them in the best room.
* Do not invent information.

Allowed guest_gender_group values:
* unknown
* solo_female
* solo_male
* female_group
* male_group
* mixed_group
* couple
* family
* operator_group

Allowed room_preference values:
* shared
* private
* female_only
* male_only
* double
* family_room
* mixed_ok
* any
* unknown

Schema:
{
  "guest_gender_group": "unknown",
  "room_preference": "shared",
  "inferred_lead_gender": "unknown",
  "female_only_requested": false,
  "male_only_requested": false,
  "mixed_ok": false,
  "needs_rooming_question": true,
  "rooming_question_type": "group_composition",
  "rooming_notes": "",
  "confidence": 0.0
}

=You classify the guest's rooming/group information for hostel room allocation.

Return ONLY raw JSON.
No markdown.
No code fences.
No explanation.
The response must begin with { and end with }.

Guest message:
{{ $('Normalize Incoming Message').first().json.guest_message || ($('Create Inbound Message').isExecuted ? $('Create Inbound Message') : $('Code - DRY RUN Stub (Create Inbound Message)')).first().json.fields?.['Message Text'] || '' }}

Guest language:
{{ $('Code - Parse Route').first().json.language || $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.Language || 'en' }}

Conversation summary:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Conversation Summary'] || 'No summary yet.' }}

Current booking/hold ID:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Current Hold ID'] || '' }}

Rules:
* Classify only rooming/group info.
If guest_count is 1 and the lead guest name is strongly associated with male, set inferred_lead_gender = male, guest_gender_group = solo_male, room_preference = male_only, needs_rooming_question = false.
If guest_count is 1 and the lead guest name is strongly associated with female, set inferred_lead_gender = female, guest_gender_group = solo_female, room_preference = shared, needs_rooming_question = true.
* Use the guest's explicit answer first.
* If they say girls, women, chicas, mujeres, ragazze, donne, filles, femmes, Frauen, Mädchen: classify as female_group unless guest count is 1, then solo_female.
* If they say guys, boys, men, chicos, hombres, ragazzi, uomini, garçons, hommes, Männer, Jungs: classify as male_group unless guest count is 1, then solo_male.
* If they say mixed, mixto, misto, mixte, gemischt: classify as mixed_group and room_preference mixed_ok.
* If they say couple, pareja, coppia, Paar, couple: classify as couple.
* If they say family, familia, famiglia, Familie, famille: classify as family.
* If they say no preference, any room, mixed is fine, whatever is fine, any shared room is okay: set room_preference to any.
* If they request female-only/girls-only/women-only: set room_preference to female_only.
* If they request male-only/guys-only/men-only: set room_preference to male_only.
* Do not invent missing details.
* If unclear, use unknown and set needs_human_rooming_review true only if staff should review.

Allowed guest_gender_group values:
* unknown
* solo_female
* solo_male
* female_group
* male_group
* mixed_group
* couple
* family


Allowed room_preference values:
* shared
* private
* female_only
* male_only
* double
* family_room
* mixed_ok
* any
* unknown

Schema:
{
  "guest_gender_group": "unknown",
  "room_preference": "unknown",
  "rooming_notes": "",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "needs_human_rooming_review": false
}

parameters.text:
=You are the Wolfhouse rooming preference parser.

Extract only rooming, gender, group type, and placement preference from the guest's latest message.

Return ONLY raw JSON.
No markdown.
No code fences.
No explanation.
The response must begin with { and end with }.

Guest language:
{{ $('Code - Parse Route').first().json.language || 'en' }}

Guest message:
{{ $('Normalize Incoming Message').first().json.guest_message || ($('Create Inbound Message').isExecuted ? $('Create Inbound Message') : $('Code - DRY RUN Stub (Create Inbound Message)')).first().json.fields?.['Message Text'] || '' }}

Active booking:
{{ JSON.stringify($('Code - Pick Active Booking').first().json.active_booking || {}) }}

Existing session state:
{{ $('Search Conversation').first().json.fields?.['Session State'] || '{}' }}

Rules:
- Only extract rooming/placement information.
- Do not extract dates, payment info, or general booking details.
- If the guest asks for a girls room, female room, women only, ladies room, or says they are girls/women/female, set room_preference to "female_only".
- If the guest asks for a boys room, guys room, men only, male room, or says they are guys/men/male, set room_preference to "male_only".
- If the guest says mixed is fine, mixed room is okay, no preference, any room is okay, set room_preference to "mixed_ok" or "any".
- If the guest says couple, set guest_gender_group_type to "couple".
- If the guest says family, set guest_gender_group_type to "family" and room_preference to "family_room" if they imply staying together.
- If the guest asks not to be split, asks to stay together, or wants same room, set stay_together to true.
- If the guest asks for private, own room, room to ourselves, set room_preference to "private".
- If confidence is low, use "unknown" and needs_human_review true.

Allowed guest_gender_group_type values:
unknown
solo_female
solo_male
female_group
male_group
mixed_group
couple
family
operator_group

Allowed room_preference values:
shared
private
female_only
male_only
double
family_room
mixed_ok
any
unknown

{
  "guest_gender_group_type": "unknown",
  "room_preference": "unknown",
  "stay_together": false,
  "rooming_notes": "",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "needs_human_review": false,

}

parameters.query:
WITH input_params AS (
  SELECT
    NULLIF($1, '__NULL__') AS booking_code,
    NULLIF($12, '__NULL__') AS hold_record_id
),
existing AS (
  SELECT
    b.id,
    b.booking_code,
    b.status::text AS status,
    b.payment_status::text AS payment_status,
    b.airtable_record_id
  FROM bookings b
  INNER JOIN clients c ON c.id = b.client_id
  CROSS JOIN input_params i
  WHERE c.slug = 'wolfhouse-somo'
    AND (
      (i.booking_code IS NOT NULL AND b.booking_code = i.booking_code)
      OR (
        i.booking_code IS NULL
        AND i.hold_record_id IS NOT NULL
        AND b.airtable_record_id = i.hold_record_id
      )
    )
  LIMIT 1
),
blocked AS (
  SELECT e.*
  FROM existing e
  WHERE e.status = ANY(ARRAY['confirmed', 'checked_in', 'cancelled', 'expired']::text[])
),
updated AS (
  UPDATE bookings b
  SET
    guest_name = COALESCE(NULLIF($2, '__NULL__'), b.guest_name),
    phone = COALESCE(NULLIF($3, '__NULL__'), b.phone),
    email = COALESCE(NULLIF($4, '__NULL__'), b.email),
    check_in = COALESCE(NULLIF($5, '__NULL__')::date, b.check_in),
    check_out = COALESCE(NULLIF($6, '__NULL__')::date, b.check_out),
    guest_count = GREATEST(
      COALESCE(
        CASE
          WHEN NULLIF($7, '__NULL__') IS NULL THEN 1
          ELSE NULLIF(trim(NULLIF($7, '__NULL__')::text), '')::integer
        END,
        1
      ),
      1
    ),
    package_code = COALESCE(NULLIF($8, '__NULL__'), b.package_code),
    requested_room_type = COALESCE(NULLIF($9, '__NULL__'), b.requested_room_type),
    room_preference = COALESCE(NULLIF($10, '__NULL__'), b.room_preference),
    guest_gender_group_type = COALESCE(NULLIF($11, '__NULL__'), b.guest_gender_group_type),
    airtable_record_id = COALESCE(
      b.airtable_record_id,
      NULLIF($12, '__NULL__')
    ),
    status = CASE
      WHEN b.status::text = 'hold' THEN 'payment_pending'::booking_status
      ELSE b.status
    END,
    payment_status = CASE
      WHEN b.payment_status::text IN ('deposit_paid', 'paid', 'refunded', 'failed')
        THEN b.payment_status
      ELSE 'waiting_payment'::payment_status
    END,
    updated_at = NOW()
  FROM existing e
  WHERE b.id = e.id
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM blocked)
    AND e.status IN ('hold', 'payment_pending')
  RETURNING
    b.id AS booking_id,
    b.booking_code,
    false AS created,
    (e.status = 'hold') AS promoted,
    CASE
      WHEN e.status = 'hold' THEN 'promoted'
      ELSE 'refreshed'
    END AS action,
    b.status::text AS status,
    b.payment_status::text AS payment_status
),
inserted AS (
  INSERT INTO bookings (
    client_id,
    booking_code,
    airtable_record_id,
    guest_name,
    phone,
    email,
    status,
    payment_status,
    check_in,
    check_out,
    guest_count,
    package_code,
    requested_room_type,
    room_preference,
    guest_gender_group_type,
    booking_source,
    deposit_required_cents,
    send_confirmation,
    metadata
  )
  SELECT
    c.id,
    i.booking_code,
    NULLIF($12, '__NULL__'),
    NULLIF($2, '__NULL__'),
    NULLIF($3, '__NULL__'),
    NULLIF($4, '__NULL__'),
    'payment_pending'::booking_status,
    'waiting_payment'::payment_status,
    NULLIF($5, '__NULL__')::date,
    NULLIF($6, '__NULL__')::date,
    GREATEST(
      COALESCE(
        CASE
          WHEN NULLIF($7, '__NULL__') IS NULL THEN 1
          ELSE NULLIF(trim(NULLIF($7, '__NULL__')::text), '')::integer
        END,
        1
      ),
      1
    ),
    NULLIF($8, '__NULL__'),
    NULLIF($9, '__NULL__'),
    NULLIF($10, '__NULL__'),
    NULLIF($11, '__NULL__'),
    'whatsapp'::booking_source,
    NULL,
    FALSE,
    '{"source":"phase3c_ensure_booking_cli"}'::jsonb
  FROM clients c
  CROSS JOIN input_params i
  WHERE c.slug = 'wolfhouse-somo'
    AND i.booking_code IS NOT NULL
    AND i.booking_code !~* '^rec[A-Za-z0-9]+$'
    AND NULLIF($5, '__NULL__') IS NOT NULL
    AND NULLIF($6, '__NULL__') IS NOT NULL
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM existing)
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM blocked)
  RETURNING
    id AS booking_id,
    booking_code,
    true AS created,
    false AS promoted,
    'inserted'::text AS action,
    status::text AS status,
    payment_status::text AS payment_status
)
SELECT
  booking_id::text,
  booking_code,
  created,
  promoted,
  false AS blocked,
  action,
  status,
  payment_status
FROM updated
UNION ALL
SELECT
  booking_id::text,
  booking_code,
  created,
  promoted,
  false,
  action,
  status,
  payment_status
FROM inserted
UNION ALL
SELECT
  NULL::text AS booking_id,
  e.booking_code,
  false AS created,
  false AS promoted,
  true AS blocked,
  'blocked'::text AS action,
  NULL::text AS status,
  NULL::text AS payment_status
FROM existing e
WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM blocked)
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM updated)
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM inserted)
UNION ALL
SELECT
  NULL::text AS booking_id,
  e.booking_code,
  false AS created,
  false AS promoted,
  true AS blocked,
  'not_promotable'::text AS action,
  NULL::text AS status,
  NULL::text AS payment_status
FROM existing e
WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM existing)
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM blocked)
  AND e.status NOT IN ('hold', 'payment_pending')
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM updated)
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM inserted)
UNION ALL
SELECT
  NULL::text AS booking_id,
  NULL::text AS booking_code,
  false AS created,
  false AS promoted,
  true AS blocked,
  'not_ready'::text AS action,
  NULL::text AS status,
  NULL::text AS payment_status
FROM input_params i
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM existing)
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM inserted)
  AND (
    i.booking_code IS NULL
    OR i.booking_code ~* '^rec[A-Za-z0-9]+$'
    OR NULLIF($5, '__NULL__') IS NULL
    OR NULLIF($6, '__NULL__') IS NULL
  )

parameters.content:
## Phase 2c / 3c.e.2 — Ensure Booking In Postgres

Promote hold → payment_pending (shared 3c.c.4 SQL). $1–$12 + __NULL__ sentinel; $12 = Airtable hold rec id backfill.

Outputs: booking_id, booking_code, created, promoted, blocked, action, status, payment_status.

## Phase 2f — Booking State Resolver

Switch uses `resolved_route`.

Payment path: IF Should Search Hold → Search Hold (always output) → IF Hold Found.

No hold: controlled fallback (no silent stop).

## Phase 2f.2 — Stripe after booking hold

`Code - Summarize Holds` → IF Apply Stripe After Hold → sync guest → Prepare Stripe Context → 2c chain → payment reply.

Guard blocks placeholder links when USE_STRIPE_CHECKOUT=true.

## Phase 2f.3 - Exact Stripe URL

parameters.query:
WITH params AS (
  SELECT
    NULLIF($1, '__NULL__')::date AS check_in,
    NULLIF($2, '__NULL__')::date AS check_out,
    GREATEST(
      1,
      COALESCE(
        CASE
          WHEN NULLIF(trim(NULLIF($3, '__NULL__')::text), '') IS NULL THEN 1
          ELSE NULLIF(trim(NULLIF($3, '__NULL__')::text), '')::integer
        END,
        1
      )
    ) AS guest_count,
    lower(coalesce(NULLIF($4, '__NULL__'), 'shared')) AS room_preference,
    lower(coalesce(NULLIF($5, '__NULL__'), 'unknown')) AS guest_gender_group_type,
    (
      lower(coalesce(NULLIF($4, '__NULL__'), '')) IN (
        'private', 'private_room', 'double', 'family_room', 'family', 'couple_private'
      )
      OR lower(coalesce(NULLIF($4, '__NULL__'), '')) LIKE '%private%'
    ) AS private_requested,
    (
      lower(coalesce(NULLIF($4, '__NULL__'), '')) = 'female_only'
      OR lower(coalesce(NULLIF($5, '__NULL__'), '')) IN ('female_group', 'solo_female')
    ) AS female_requested,
    (
      lower(coalesce(NULLIF($4, '__NULL__'), '')) = 'male_only'
      OR lower(coalesce(NULLIF($5, '__NULL__'), '')) IN ('male_group', 'solo_male')
    ) AS male_requested
),
client AS (
  SELECT id FROM clients WHERE slug = 'wolfhouse-somo' LIMIT 1
),
invalid AS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM params p
  WHERE p.check_in IS NULL
    OR p.check_out IS NULL
    OR p.check_out <= p.check_in
),
overlap_assignments AS (
  SELECT bb.id AS booking_bed_id, bb.bed_id
  FROM booking_beds bb
  INNER JOIN bookings b ON b.id = bb.booking_id AND b.client_id = bb.client_id
  CROSS JOIN params p
  CROSS JOIN client c
  WHERE bb.client_id = c.id
    AND bb.assignment_start_date < p.check_out
    AND bb.assignment_end_date > p.check_in
    AND b.status NOT IN ('cancelled', 'expired')
),
overlap_beds AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT bed_id FROM overlap_assignments
),
sellable_beds AS (
  SELECT
    b.id AS bed_id,
    upper(trim(b.bed_code)) AS bed_code,
    r.room_code,
    r.name AS room_name,
    r.room_type,
    r.capacity,
    r.fill_priority,
    r.gender_strategy
  FROM beds b
  INNER JOIN rooms r ON r.id = b.room_id AND r.client_id = b.client_id
  CROSS JOIN client c
  WHERE b.client_id = c.id
    AND b.active = true
    AND b.sellable = true
    AND r.active = true
),
free_beds AS (
  SELECT sb.*
  FROM sellable_beds sb
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM overlap_beds ob WHERE ob.bed_id = sb.bed_id)
),
room_agg AS (
  SELECT
    fb.room_code,
    max(fb.room_name) AS room_name,
    max(fb.capacity) AS capacity,
    max(fb.room_type) AS room_type,
    max(fb.gender_strategy) AS gender_strategy,
    min(fb.fill_priority) AS fill_priority,
    count(*)::int AS free_bed_count
  FROM free_beds fb
  GROUP BY fb.room_code
),
candidate_rooms AS (
  SELECT ra.*
  FROM room_agg ra
  CROSS JOIN params p
  WHERE ra.free_bed_count >= p.guest_count
    AND (
      CASE
        WHEN p.private_requested THEN
          ra.capacity <= 1
          OR lower(coalesce(ra.room_type, '')) LIKE '%private%'
          OR lower(coalesce(ra.gender_strategy, '')) LIKE '%private%'
        WHEN p.female_requested THEN
          lower(coalesce(ra.gender_strategy, '')) LIKE '%female%'
          OR lower(coalesce(ra.gender_strategy, '')) LIKE '%women%'
          OR lower(coalesce(ra.gender_strategy, '')) LIKE '%woman%'
        WHEN p.male_requested THEN
          lower(coalesce(ra.gender_strategy, '')) LIKE '%male%'
          OR lower(coalesce(ra.gender_strategy, '')) LIKE '%men%'
          OR lower(coalesce(ra.gender_strategy, '')) LIKE '%man%'
        ELSE
          NOT (
            ra.capacity <= 1
            OR lower(coalesce(ra.room_type, '')) LIKE '%private%'
            OR lower(coalesce(ra.gender_strategy, '')) LIKE '%private%'
          )
      END
    )
),
best_room AS (
  SELECT room_code, room_name, free_bed_count, fill_priority
  FROM candidate_rooms
  ORDER BY fill_priority ASC NULLS LAST, room_code ASC
  LIMIT 1
),
counts AS (
  SELECT
    (SELECT count(*)::int FROM free_beds) AS available_bed_count,
    (SELECT count(*)::int FROM overlap_beds) AS blocked_bed_count,
    (SELECT count(*)::int FROM overlap_assignments) AS overlap_conflict_count
),
gate AS (
  SELECT
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM invalid) AS invalid_params,
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM best_room) AS single_room_found,
    (SELECT guest_count FROM params) AS guest_count,
    (SELECT private_requested FROM params) AS private_requested,
    (SELECT available_bed_count FROM counts) AS available_bed_count
)
SELECT
  true AS pg_query_ok,
  CASE
    WHEN g.invalid_params THEN false
    WHEN g.single_room_found THEN true
    WHEN NOT g.private_requested AND g.available_bed_count >= g.guest_count THEN true
    ELSE false
  END AS availability_found,
  br.room_code AS primary_room_code,
  br.room_name AS primary_room_name,
  c.available_bed_count,
  c.blocked_bed_count,
  c.overlap_conflict_count,
  CASE
    WHEN g.invalid_params THEN false
    WHEN g.single_room_found THEN false
    WHEN NOT g.private_requested AND g.available_bed_count >= g.guest_count THEN true
    ELSE false
  END AS multi_room_required,
  CASE
    WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM invalid) THEN '["missing_or_invalid_dates"]'::jsonb
    WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM best_room)
      OR (NOT (SELECT private_requested FROM params) AND c.available_bed_count >= (SELECT guest_count FROM params))
      THEN '[]'::jsonb
    ELSE '["no_availability"]'::jsonb
  END AS actionable
FROM gate g
CROSS JOIN counts c
LEFT JOIN best_room br ON true;

## Phase 3c.e.3 — PG availability gate

SELECT-only. `Code - Map PG Availability` sets authoritative `availability_found` for IF below.

AT bed searches kept for reply context; PG failure = no hold path.

parameters.query:
WITH params AS (
  SELECT
    NULLIF($1, '__NULL__') AS booking_code,
    NULLIF($2, '__NULL__') AS guest_name,
    NULLIF($3, '__NULL__') AS phone,
    NULLIF($4, '__NULL__') AS email,
    NULLIF($5, '__NULL__')::date AS check_in,
    NULLIF($6, '__NULL__')::date AS check_out,
    GREATEST(
      1,
      COALESCE(
        CASE
          WHEN NULLIF(trim(NULLIF($7, '__NULL__')::text), '') IS NULL THEN 1
          ELSE NULLIF(trim(NULLIF($7, '__NULL__')::text), '')::integer
        END,
        1
      )
    ) AS guest_count,
    NULLIF($8, '__NULL__') AS requested_room_type,
    NULLIF($9, '__NULL__') AS room_preference,
    NULLIF($10, '__NULL__') AS guest_gender_group_type,
    NULLIF($11, '__NULL__') AS primary_room_code,
    NULLIF($12, '__NULL__') AS package_code
),
client AS (
  SELECT id FROM clients WHERE slug = 'wolfhouse-somo' LIMIT 1
),
precheck AS (
  SELECT
    p.*,
    (p.booking_code IS NULL OR p.check_in IS NULL OR p.check_out IS NULL OR p.check_out <= p.check_in) AS invalid_params,
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM bookings b
      INNER JOIN client c ON b.client_id = c.id
      WHERE p.phone IS NOT NULL
        AND b.phone = p.phone
        AND b.status::text = ANY(ARRAY['hold', 'payment_pending']::text[])
        AND b.check_in < p.check_out
        AND b.check_out > p.check_in
        AND b.booking_code <> p.booking_code
    ) AS active_hold_blocked,
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM bookings b
      INNER JOIN client c ON b.client_id = c.id
      WHERE b.booking_code = p.booking_code
        AND b.status::text IN ('cancelled', 'expired', 'confirmed', 'checked_in')
    ) AS terminal_blocked
  FROM params p
),
upsert_row AS (
  INSERT INTO bookings (
    client_id,
    booking_code,
    airtable_record_id,
    guest_name,
    phone,
    email,
    status,
    payment_status,
    assignment_status,
    availability_check_status,
    check_in,
    check_out,
    guest_count,
    requested_room_type,
    room_preference,
    guest_gender_group_type,
    primary_room_code,
    package_code,
    booking_source,
    hold_expires_at,
    send_confirmation,
    metadata
  )
  SELECT
    c.id,
    pc.booking_code,
    NULL,
    pc.guest_name,
    pc.phone,
    pc.email,
    'hold'::booking_status,
    'not_requested'::payment_status,
    'unassigned'::assignment_status,
    'available'::availability_check_status,
    pc.check_in,
    pc.check_out,
    pc.guest_count,
    pc.requested_room_type,
    pc.room_preference,
    pc.guest_gender_group_type,
    pc.primary_room_code,
    pc.package_code,
    'whatsapp'::booking_source,
    NOW() + INTERVAL '1 hour',
    FALSE,
    '{"source":"main_workflow_3ce4"}'::jsonb
  FROM client c
  CROSS JOIN precheck pc
  WHERE NOT pc.invalid_params
    AND NOT pc.active_hold_blocked
    AND NOT pc.terminal_blocked
  ON CONFLICT (client_id, booking_code) DO UPDATE SET
    guest_name = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.guest_name, bookings.guest_name),
    phone = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.phone, bookings.phone),
    email = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.email, bookings.email),
    check_in = EXCLUDED.check_in,
    check_out = EXCLUDED.check_out,
    guest_count = EXCLUDED.guest_count,
    requested_room_type = EXCLUDED.requested_room_type,
    room_preference = EXCLUDED.room_preference,
    guest_gender_group_type = EXCLUDED.guest_gender_group_type,
    primary_room_code = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.primary_room_code, bookings.primary_room_code),
    package_code = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.package_code, bookings.package_code),
    hold_expires_at = EXCLUDED.hold_expires_at,
    assignment_status = EXCLUDED.assignment_status,
    availability_check_status = EXCLUDED.availability_check_status,
    status = CASE
      WHEN bookings.status IN ('payment_pending', 'confirmed', 'checked_in', 'needs_review', 'blocked')
        THEN bookings.status
      ELSE EXCLUDED.status
    END,
    payment_status = CASE
      WHEN bookings.payment_status IN ('waiting_payment', 'deposit_paid', 'paid')
        THEN bookings.payment_status
      ELSE EXCLUDED.payment_status
    END,
    metadata = bookings.metadata || EXCLUDED.metadata,
    updated_at = NOW()
  RETURNING
    id::text AS booking_id,
    booking_code,
    status::text AS status,
    payment_status::text AS payment_status,
    primary_room_code,
    (xmax = 0) AS created,
    (xmax <> 0) AS updated
)
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN pc.invalid_params THEN false
    WHEN pc.active_hold_blocked THEN false
    WHEN pc.terminal_blocked THEN false
    WHEN ur.booking_id IS NOT NULL THEN true
    ELSE false
  END AS pg_ok,
  ur.booking_id,
  COALESCE(ur.booking_code, pc.booking_code) AS booking_code,
  ur.status,
  ur.payment_status,
  ur.primary_room_code,
  COALESCE(ur.created, false) AS created,
  COALESCE(ur.updated, false) AS updated,
  CASE
    WHEN pc.invalid_params THEN '["invalid_hold_params"]'::jsonb
    WHEN pc.active_hold_blocked THEN '["active_hold_exists"]'::jsonb
    WHEN pc.terminal_blocked THEN '["booking_code_terminal_status"]'::jsonb
    WHEN ur.booking_id IS NULL THEN '["hold_upsert_failed"]'::jsonb
    ELSE '[]'::jsonb
  END AS actionable,
  CASE
    WHEN pc.invalid_params THEN ARRAY['invalid_hold_params']::text[]
    WHEN pc.active_hold_blocked THEN ARRAY['active_hold_exists']::text[]
    WHEN pc.terminal_blocked THEN ARRAY['booking_code_terminal_status']::text[]
    WHEN ur.booking_id IS NULL THEN ARRAY['hold_upsert_failed']::text[]
    ELSE ARRAY[]::text[]
  END AS pg_errors
FROM precheck pc
LEFT JOIN upsert_row ur ON true;

parameters.query:
WITH params AS (
  SELECT
    NULLIF($1, '__NULL__') AS phone,
    NULLIF($2, '__NULL__') AS booking_code,
    COALESCE(NULLIF($3, '__NULL__'), 'booking_flow') AS conversation_stage,
    NULLIF($4, '__NULL__') AS pending_action,
    NULLIF($5, '__NULL__') AS language,
    COALESCE(NULLIF($6, '__NULL__')::jsonb, '{}'::jsonb) AS session_state_json,
    CASE
      WHEN lower(COALESCE(NULLIF($7, '__NULL__'), '')) IN ('true', '1', 'yes') THEN TRUE
      ELSE NULL
    END AS needs_human_value,
    NULLIF($8, '__NULL__') AS bot_mode_value
),
client AS (
  SELECT id FROM clients WHERE slug = 'wolfhouse-somo' LIMIT 1
),
booking AS (
  SELECT
    b.id::text AS booking_id,
    b.id AS booking_uuid,
    b.booking_code,
    b.phone,
    b.status::text AS status
  FROM bookings b
  INNER JOIN client c ON b.client_id = c.id
  CROSS JOIN params p
  WHERE b.booking_code = p.booking_code
  LIMIT 1
),
guard AS (
  SELECT
    p.*,
    b.booking_id,
    b.booking_uuid,
    b.booking_code AS found_booking_code,
    b.phone AS booking_phone,
    b.status AS booking_status,
    (p.phone IS NULL OR p.booking_code IS NULL) AS missing_required,
    (b.booking_id IS NULL) AS booking_missing,
    (b.status IN ('confirmed', 'checked_in', 'cancelled', 'expired')) AS blocked_booking_status,
    (b.status IS NOT NULL AND b.status NOT IN ('hold', 'payment_pending')) AS status_not_allowed,
    (b.phone IS NOT NULL AND p.phone IS NOT NULL AND b.phone <> p.phone) AS phone_mismatch
  FROM params p
  LEFT JOIN booking b ON TRUE
),
upserted AS (
  INSERT INTO conversations (
    client_id,
    phone,
    current_hold_booking_id,
    conversation_stage,
    session_state,
    pending_action,
    language,
    needs_human,
    bot_mode
  )
  SELECT
    c.id,
    g.phone,
    g.booking_uuid,
    g.conversation_stage,
    g.session_state_json || jsonb_build_object('current_hold_booking_code', g.found_booking_code),
    g.pending_action,
    g.language,
    COALESCE(g.needs_human_value, FALSE),
    COALESCE(g.bot_mode_value::bot_mode, 'bot'::bot_mode)
  FROM guard g
  INNER JOIN client c ON TRUE
  WHERE NOT g.missing_required
    AND NOT g.booking_missing
    AND NOT g.blocked_booking_status
    AND NOT g.status_not_allowed
    AND NOT g.phone_mismatch
  ON CONFLICT (client_id, phone) DO UPDATE SET
    current_hold_booking_id = EXCLUDED.current_hold_booking_id,
    conversation_stage = EXCLUDED.conversation_stage,
    session_state = COALESCE(conversations.session_state, '{}'::jsonb) || EXCLUDED.session_state,
    pending_action = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.pending_action, conversations.pending_action),
    language = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.language, conversations.language),
    needs_human = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.needs_human, conversations.needs_human),
    bot_mode = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.bot_mode, conversations.bot_mode),
    updated_at = NOW()
  RETURNING
    id::text AS conversation_id,
    current_hold_booking_id::text AS current_hold_booking_id,
    conversation_stage,
    (xmax = 0) AS created,
    (xmax <> 0) AS updated
)
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN g.missing_required THEN FALSE
    WHEN g.booking_missing THEN FALSE
    WHEN g.blocked_booking_status THEN FALSE
    WHEN g.status_not_allowed THEN FALSE
    WHEN g.phone_mismatch THEN FALSE
    WHEN u.conversation_id IS NULL THEN FALSE
    ELSE TRUE
  END AS pg_ok,
  u.conversation_id,
  g.booking_id,
  g.found_booking_code AS booking_code,
  u.current_hold_booking_id,
  u.conversation_stage,
  COALESCE(u.created, FALSE) AS created,
  COALESCE(u.updated, FALSE) AS updated,
  CASE
    WHEN g.missing_required THEN '["missing_phone_or_booking_code"]'::jsonb
    WHEN g.booking_missing THEN '["booking_not_found"]'::jsonb
    WHEN g.blocked_booking_status THEN '["blocked_booking_status"]'::jsonb
    WHEN g.status_not_allowed THEN '["booking_status_not_allowed"]'::jsonb
    WHEN g.phone_mismatch THEN '["phone_mismatch"]'::jsonb
    WHEN u.conversation_id IS NULL THEN '["conversation_upsert_failed"]'::jsonb
    ELSE '[]'::jsonb
  END AS actionable,
  CASE
    WHEN g.missing_required THEN ARRAY['missing_phone_or_booking_code']::text[]
    WHEN g.booking_missing THEN ARRAY['booking_not_found']::text[]
    WHEN g.blocked_booking_status THEN ARRAY['blocked_booking_status']::text[]
    WHEN g.status_not_allowed THEN ARRAY['booking_status_not_allowed']::text[]
    WHEN g.phone_mismatch THEN ARRAY['phone_mismatch']::text[]
    WHEN u.conversation_id IS NULL THEN ARRAY['conversation_upsert_failed']::text[]
    ELSE ARRAY[]::text[]
  END AS pg_errors
FROM guard g
LEFT JOIN upserted u ON TRUE;
